7.reason: [Loop condition uses newline; if input lacks newline, loop may continue into undefined memory or skip entirely, causing empty output or runtime error. Should use s[i] != '\0' or i < strlen(s).]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Loop condition uses newline; if input lacks newline, loop may continue into undefined memory or skip entirely, causing empty output or runtime error. Should use s[i] != '\0' or i < strlen(s).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Loop condition assumes newline always present; may cause out‑of‑bounds access if no newline.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition s[i]!='\n' causes a Runtime Error if the input string does not contain a newline character (e.g., last line of input without newline or empty input), leading to out-of-bounds access. It should also check for the null terminator '\0'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `s[i]!='\n'` causes a Runtime Error if the input string does not contain a newline character (e.g., last line of file without newline or empty input), leading to out-of-bounds access. It should also check for the null terminator `\0`.

11.reason: [This condition accesses s[i+1] and s[i-1] without checking if i is at the start or end of the string, which can lead to reading uninitialized memory or out-of-bounds access when i=0 or i points to the last character before '\n'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without boundary checks; can cause out‑of‑bounds if hyphen at start or end.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition accesses s[i+1] and s[i-1] without checking if i is at the start or end of the string, which can lead to reading uninitialized memory or out-of-bounds access when i=0 or i points to the last character before '\n'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition accesses s[i+1] and s[i-1] without checking if i is at the start or end of the string, which can lead to out-of-bounds memory access (undefined behavior) when i=0 or i points to the last character before '\n'.]

13.reason: [Same issue: accessing s[i-1] when i could be 0 leads to reading before the start of the array.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: accessing s[i-1] when i could be 0 leads to reading before the start of the array.]

18.reason: [Same out-of-bounds risk for s[i-1] and s[i+1].]
